title: Slow Coffee Sky
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
7:12, kettle singing in the kitchen light
Steam on the window writes a crooked line
Rain on the crosswalk, silver on black
My hoodie smells like detergent and toast
Laptop hums low like a subway floor
Calendar squares with the corners worn
Blue pen stain on the side of my hand
Neighbor’s footsteps drift down the hall
I take one breath, let the morning in
Tiny green lamp, soft on the desk
The city is loud but my room is kind
I start this day on a quiet beat
[Chorus]
Keep it easy, keep it moving
Keep it easy, keep it moving
One small task and then another
Click by click, I find my rhythm
Keep it easy, keep it moving
Shoulders lower, heartbeat steady
In this gentle kind of motion
I can go all day
[Verse 2]
2:03, ice melts in a paper cup
Lemon slice turns in a ring of gold
Printer spits warm sheets, fresh ink air
Sun crawls slowly across my keys
Phone lights up with a simple “you got this”
Elevator dings from the office core
Mint gum, cold water, reset my head
Traffic below in a distant wash
I misspell one word, then smile and fix
No drama, no crash, just forward flow
A sax line leaks from someone’s headphones
And somehow my deadline starts to dance
[Bridge]
If the pace gets heavy, count to four
One for the breath and two for the room
Three for the light on the window frame
Four for the hands that know what to do
No need to rush to prove I’m alive
I’m building something line by line
Let the late sun turn the wall tangerine
I’m still right here, still in time
[Chorus]
Keep it easy, keep it moving
Keep it easy, keep it moving
Now the drums are a little brighter
Pulse and piano lifting higher
Keep it easy, keep it glowing
Keep it easy, keep it glowing
Evening settles, screen goes amber
I close the day with a steady fire
styles: Soft husky tenor lead with airy female doubles and stacked chorus harmonies, 94 BPM mellow American pop, intimate intro (30%) growing with brushed drums/bass/piano layers to a warm, uplifting final-chorus peak (95-100%).
